track_listing.track: Can I Sit Next To You?

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Hi there. Good evening to you. TV on Radio Thomas Vance here. Welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
This evening the only session that AC DC recorded for Radio 1 way back in 1976. We also have the first session recorded by Syd Barrett.
Original Floyd member. That one was put together in the 70s. Actually in 1970. Also Praying Mantis.
We've got classic cuts from Motorhead, Ted Nugent, Todd Rundgren, among others,
and new releases by Frank Zappa, Wishbone Ash, and The Pretenders.
My show from Anne Nightingale tonight.
Lucky lady got to interview that Miss Hine from The Pretenders.
Also, the Friday Night Connection later, but right now you're not in bad company.
[Music]
That's the first of three by Praying Mantis tonight.
That's the session we put together, what, round about last, well, the 31st of the 10th of 1979, to be precise.
It was called Captured City.
And prior to that, something that came out in 1974
on the album Bad Company by Bad Company,
the track was Rock Steady.
And that was for John Wilson of Five Somerdale Gardens,
Bramley and Leith.
Quick reminder that if there are any tracks
that you remember from the dim and distant past,
if you'd like us to get out of the BBC Record Library,
just write to us here at the Friday Rock Show,
BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW,
and we'll go digging.
as indeed we dug for this
because many people wanted to hear it
the 1976 session from ACDC

and I really thought they were a joke until I went to see them.
And then I changed my mind.

that has recorded product to be played in the home,

This one comes from Martin Lawson,
who lives at 19 The Old Garden, Chipstead in Seven Oaks in Kent.
